name: react-modernization
description: Upgrade React applications to latest versions, migrate from class components to hooks, and adopt concurrent features. Use when modernizing React codebases, migrating to React Hooks, or upgrading to latest React versions.
capabilities: Guide React version upgrades from 16 to 18 with breaking change analysis
Migrate class components to functional components with hooks
Apply codemods for automated code transformations
Implement React 18 concurrent features like Suspense and transitions
Optimize performance with useMemo, useCallback, and code splitting
Support TypeScript migration for React components
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 48 patterns but all are false positives. This skill contains documentation-only markdown files with React code examples. Dynamic imports are standard React lazy() patterns, shell commands are documentation for codemod usage, and fetch calls are example code snippets. No executable code exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Legacy React codebases become difficult to maintain and miss performance improvements. This skill guides you through version upgrades, hooks migration, and adopting React 18 concurrent features.
userTitle: Modernize React Applications to Latest Versions
limitations: Cannot execute codemods directly - provides commands for user to run
Does not test migrated code - users must verify functionality
Focuses on React-specific changes, not broader application architecture
Requires user to have codebase access and development environment

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers maintaining legacy React codebases
useCases.title: Legacy React Application Upgrade
useCases.description: Upgrade an older React 16 application to React 18 with automatic batching and concurrent features

useCases.targetUser: Teams standardizing on React hooks patterns
useCases.title: Class to Hooks Migration
useCases.description: Convert class components with lifecycle methods to modern functional components using useState and useEffect

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Implement code splitting, memoization, and Suspense boundaries for improved application performance

promptTemplates.title: Basic Hooks Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this React class component to use hooks. Identify state variables and lifecycle methods to transform into useState and useEffect.

promptTemplates.title: Version Upgrade Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my React codebase for breaking changes when upgrading from React 17 to 18. List all files that need modifications and provide a migration checklist.

promptTemplates.title: Codemod Automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate commands to run React codemods for my project. Include jscodeshift installation, specific transforms for lifecycle methods, and the new JSX transform.

promptTemplates.title: Concurrent Features Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor this search component to use useTransition for non-urgent updates. Show how to implement Suspense boundaries for data fetching with proper loading states.

bestPractices: Migrate incrementally starting with leaf components that have no children
Run comprehensive tests after each migration step to catch regressions early
Use React StrictMode to identify unsafe patterns and side effects during development
antiPatterns: Adding useMemo or useCallback without measuring actual performance problems
Forgetting to include all dependencies in useEffect dependency arrays
Mixing class component patterns with hooks in the same component

faq.question: Do I need to migrate all components at once?
faq.answer: No. React supports mixing class and functional components. Migrate incrementally, starting with simpler leaf components.

faq.question: Will codemods handle all the migration automatically?
faq.answer: Codemods automate repetitive transformations but cannot handle complex logic. Manual review and testing are still required.

faq.question: What is the minimum React version for hooks?
faq.answer: Hooks require React 16.8 or later. If on an older version, upgrade to 16.8+ first before migrating to hooks.

faq.question: Are there performance concerns with React 18 automatic batching?
faq.answer: Automatic batching improves performance by reducing re-renders. Use flushSync only if you need immediate DOM updates.

faq.question: How do I handle cleanup in useEffect?
faq.answer: Return a cleanup function from useEffect that cancels subscriptions, aborts fetch requests, or clears timers.

faq.question: Can I use Suspense for data fetching in production?
faq.answer: Suspense for data fetching is stable in React 18 but requires compatible data fetching libraries or custom implementations.
